subtitle: White Collar vs. Blue Collar vs. No Collar

winner.name: Mike Holloway
winner.runner_up: Carolyn Rivera and Will Sims II (tied)

timeline.description: Initial tribe formation with three tribes based on social class and work style
timeline.impact: 18 castaways divided into three tribes of six based on occupation: Blue Collar 'follow the rules', White Collar 'make the rules', No Collar 'break the rules'

timeline.description: Tribe Leader Decision - Leaders chose second member to make critical decision affecting tribe

details.mechanic: Each tribe selected a leader who chose another tribemate to make a decision together
details.choice_given: Leaders and partners chose between large bag of beans (Honest) or smaller bag plus Hidden Immunity Idol clue (Deceive)
details.deception: Joaquin and So chose Deceive but lied to tribe, claiming there was a third 'Neutral' option

timeline.impact: Joaquin and So received idol clue but created distrust by lying about the decision options; lie exposed at first Tribal Council

timeline.description: Masaya tribe disbanded, remaining 14 castaways redistributed into two tribes of seven

tribes_after_swap.name: Escameca
tribes_after_swap.composition: 4 Blue Collar, 2 White Collar, 1 No Collar - strong physical tribe

tribes_after_swap.name: Nagarote
tribes_after_swap.composition: 3 White Collar, 3 No Collar, 1 Blue Collar - physically weaker tribe

timeline.impact: White Collar tribe dissolved; Escameca gained significant physical advantage with Joe, Joaquin, Mike, and Rodney

timeline.description: Joaquin blindsided to break up Blue Collar duo and retain Rodney's loyalty

details.action: Mike led Blue Collars to blindside Joaquin, who had aligned with Rodney
details.voters_against: Mike, Dan, Sierra, and Joe voted out Joaquin

timeline.impact: Broke up dangerous Rodney-Joaquin pair; kept Rodney loyal to Blue Collar alliance
Individual game begins with 12 players; Blue Collar alliance versus No Collar core alliance with Shirin

timeline.description: Survivor Auction with Extra Vote advantage introduced

details.advantage_type: Extra Vote - allows player to vote twice at single Tribal Council
details.context: Mike initially refused to pay $20 for family letter to save money for advantage, then changed mind; this turn damaged his social game and made him a target

timeline.impact: First introduction of Extra Vote advantage in Survivor history; Mike's auction behavior turned tribe against him

timeline.description: Mike exposed Rodney's secret alliance, became primary target

details.action: Mike overheard Rodney's inner alliance plotting his blindside and publicly called them out
details.result: Mike's allies turned on him, making him the main target
details.rodney_alliance: Rodney had secret sub-alliance with Will, Tyler, and Carolyn to eventually take down other Blue Collars

timeline.impact: Mike became isolated underdog; forced to win immunity challenges to survive

timeline.description: Mike played Hidden Immunity Idol, blindsided Tyler

details.action: Mike played idol, negating four votes against him

timeline.impact: Mike survived elimination; took out strategic threat Tyler in blindside

timeline.description: Dan used Extra Vote advantage at Final 7, but Carolyn played idol

details.votes_cast: Dan cast two votes for Carolyn using Extra Vote
details.idol_play: Carolyn Rivera played Hidden Immunity Idol

timeline.impact: Dan's advantage backfired; Carolyn's idol play sent Dan to jury; demonstrated power of immunity idol over extra vote

timeline.description: Mike began unprecedented immunity challenge run

details.achievement: Mike won 5 of last 6 immunity challenges
details.context: After being targeted, Mike had to win immunity to survive
details.only_vulnerability: Day 33 - played Hidden Immunity Idol instead

timeline.impact: Mike's dominance in challenges carried him to Final Tribal Council despite being outnumbered
Classic tiebreaker mechanic returned; added survival skills element to endgame

timeline.description: Final Tribal Council and jury vote
timeline.historical_note: First season with three starting tribes to have representative from each original tribe in Final Three
timeline.impact: Mike Holloway crowned Sole Survivor and winner of $1,000,000 after unprecedented immunity run

season_mechanics.description: White Collar vs. Blue Collar vs. No Collar theme based on social class and work style
Leaders selected partner to make choice between Honest (more beans) or Deceive (fewer beans + idol clue)
First season to introduce Extra Vote - allows player to vote twice at single Tribal Council, offered at auction
Day 12 swap dissolved White Collar tribe (Masaya) and redistributed into two tribes of seven
Return of fire-making challenge at Final 4, last used in Survivor: Gabon
Classic auction with advantage opportunity; included family letters moment

notable_stats.unique_achievement: First season since South Pacific (Season 23) with no quits or medical evacuations
